frame_analyses.description: The needle is being loaded into the needle holder.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper loading of the needle is crucial for accurate suture placement.

frame_analyses.description: The needle is being passed through the first mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle is aligned well with slight deviation from the mark.

frame_analyses.description: The needle is exiting at the second mark on the Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Exits with slight deviation from the intended mark.

frame_analyses.description: The first throw of the knot is being tied.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: A double throw is being made for a surgeon's knot.

frame_analyses.description: The second single throw is being made, switching hand holding the needle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch observed, single throw made correctly.

frame_analyses.description: The third single throw is being made, switching hand again.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch observed, single throw made smoothly.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture ends are being cut after the knot is secure.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ends cut close to the knot, no fraying observed.

knot_assessments.notes: Good switch of hands, throw executed well.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: The slit is completely closed without visible gaps.

technique_summary: Overall, the technique demonstrated good hand coordination and secure knot tying. Minor deviations in suture placement were noted but did not affect knot integrity.
improvement_suggestions: Practice more on precise suture placement to reduce deviation.
Work on improving speed with maintaining suture integrity.
strengths: Knot tying was secure and well-executed.
